root_text.an4.94:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, na lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.94:1.4: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī hoti adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, na lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa.
root_text.an4.94:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na ceva lābhī hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa na ca lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.94:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.94:2.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa na lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya so upasaṅkamitvā evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.an4.94:2.6: ‘evaṁ kho, āvuso, saṅkhārā daṭṭhabbā, evaṁ saṅkhārā sammasitabbā, evaṁ saṅkhārā vipassitabbā’ti.
root_text.an4.94:2.7: So aparena samayena lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.94:3.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya na lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa so upasaṅkamitvā evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.an4.94:3.7: ‘evaṁ kho, āvuso, cittaṁ saṇṭhapetabbaṁ, evaṁ cittaṁ sannisādetabbaṁ, evaṁ cittaṁ ekodi kātabbaṁ, evaṁ cittaṁ samādahātabban’ti.
root_text.an4.94:3.8: So aparena samaye lābhī ceva hoti adhipaññādhammavipassanāya lābhī ca ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa.
root_text.an4.94:4.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo na ceva lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa na ca lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī ceva ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya so upasaṅkamitvā evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.an4.94:4.10: ‘evaṁ kho, āvuso, cittaṁ saṇṭhapetabbaṁ, evaṁ cittaṁ sannisādetabbaṁ, evaṁ cittaṁ ekodi kātabbaṁ, evaṁ cittaṁ samādahātabbaṁ, evaṁ saṅkhārā daṭṭhabbā, evaṁ saṅkhārā sammasitabbā, evaṁ saṅkhārā vipassitabbā’ti.
root_text.an4.94:4.11: So aparena samayena lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.94:5.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena tesu ceva kusalesu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya uttari āsavānaṁ khayāya yogo karaṇīyo.
root_text.an4.94:5.2: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.94:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.94:1.3: One individual has internal serenity of heart, but not the higher wisdom of discernment of principles.
translation_text.an4.94:1.4: One individual has the higher wisdom of discernment of principles, but not internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an4.94:1.5: One individual has neither internal serenity of heart, nor the higher wisdom of discernment of principles.
translation_text.an4.94:1.6: One individual has both internal serenity of heart, and the higher wisdom of discernment of principles.
translation_text.an4.94:2.1: As for the individual who has serenity but not discernment: they should approach someone who has discernment and ask:
translation_text.an4.94:2.5: They would answer from their own experience:
translation_text.an4.94:2.6: ‘This is how conditions should be seen, comprehended, and discerned.’
translation_text.an4.94:2.7: After some time they have both serenity and discernment.
translation_text.an4.94:3.1: As for the individual who has discernment but not serenity: they should approach someone who has serenity and ask:
translation_text.an4.94:3.2: ‘Reverend, how should the mind be stilled?
translation_text.an4.94:3.5: How should it be immersed in samādhi?’
translation_text.an4.94:3.6: They would answer from their own experience:
translation_text.an4.94:3.7: ‘Reverend, this is how the mind should be stilled, settled, unified, and immersed in samādhi.’
translation_text.an4.94:3.8: After some time they have both discernment and serenity.
translation_text.an4.94:4.1: As for the individual who has neither serenity nor discernment: they should approach someone who has serenity and discernment and ask:
translation_text.an4.94:4.2: ‘Reverend, how should the mind be stilled?
translation_text.an4.94:4.5: How should it be immersed in samādhi?’
translation_text.an4.94:4.9: They would answer as they’ve seen and known:
translation_text.an4.94:4.10: ‘Reverend, this is how the mind should be stilled, settled, unified, and immersed in samādhi. And this is how conditions should be seen, comprehended, and discerned.’
translation_text.an4.94:4.11: After some time they have both serenity and discernment.
translation_text.an4.94:5.1: As for the individual who has both serenity and discernment: grounded on those skillful qualities, they should practice meditation further to end the defilements.
translation_text.an4.94:5.2: These are the four individuals found in the world.”
